heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions Across the North West
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions North West | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built home extensions for North West homes, made in Blackburn and installed in under a week. Kitchens, offices and garden rooms from EazyBase.
intro:
The North West is where EazyBase began, and it's still where we do our best work. Every extension we build starts life in our Blackburn factory, which means homeowners across the region get something nobody else can offer: the shortest possible journey from our workshop floor to your back garden. While traditional builders are still waiting on skips and scaffolding, your extension is being precision-built indoors, ready to arrive finished.

If you've been weighing up modular home extensions in North West England, the appeal is simple. We design your space with you, factory-build it in as little as four weeks, then install it on-site in under a week. That means a new kitchen, dining room, home office or playroom without months of mud, dust and disruption — something that matters when you're living in the house the whole time.

From Victorian terraces to post-war semis, we know the region's housing stock inside out, because it's the housing stock we grew up with. Explore our local pages below, or get in touch and we'll talk through what's possible for your home.

localAngles.heading: Our home region — and our fastest installs
localAngles.body: Every EazyBase extension is built in our Blackburn factory, so North West homeowners are quite literally closest to the source. Shorter delivery runs mean easier scheduling, less time between factory completion and installation day, and a team that's never far away if you want to talk something through in person. We install right across the region — and because our installers are local, they already know the streets, the housing styles and the practicalities of working here. If you'd like to see how your extension is made before you commit, you're welcome to ask about visiting the factory.

localAngles.heading: Extending North West terraces and semis
localAngles.body: The North West's streets are full of Victorian and Edwardian terraces and inter-war semis — homes with real character, but often with kitchens and living spaces that feel tight by modern standards. These are exactly the houses modular extensions suit best. A single-storey rear addition can turn a narrow galley kitchen into a proper kitchen-diner, or add the home office or playroom the original floor plan never allowed for. Because each module is built to your home's measurements in the factory, it arrives designed around your yard, your access and your existing brickwork — not a one-size-fits-all box.

localAngles.heading: Planning across the North West
localAngles.body: Planning rules are set nationally but administered by your local council, and the North West covers dozens of them — from big city authorities to smaller borough councils. The good news is that permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, meaning many of our projects don't need a full planning application at all. That said, conservation areas and certain terraced settings can change the picture, so always check with your local council before committing. We help with this as part of every project: our design team prepares the drawings and guides you through whatever route your home requires.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Anyone who's lived through a traditional build in the North West knows the weather has opinions. Rain-delayed brickwork, sodden gardens, tarpaulins flapping over half-finished walls — it all adds weeks and stress. Factory building sidesteps the problem entirely. Your extension is constructed indoors, in controlled conditions, to a consistent standard, whatever the forecast is doing outside. On-site work is compressed into less than a week, so there's only a small window where weather matters at all. For a region that earns its greenery the wet way, it's hard to think of a better fit.

faqs.q: How quickly can you install an extension in the North West?
faqs.a: Faster here than anywhere. Your extension is factory-built in Blackburn in as little as four weeks, and because the whole North West is on our doorstep, delivery and scheduling are as straightforward as they get. Once your module arrives, on-site installation typically takes under a week from start to finish.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission for a modular extension?
faqs.a: Often not. Permitted development rights frequently cover single-storey rear extensions, which is what most of our projects are. Rules vary with conservation areas and certain property types, though, so always check with your local council. Our design team prepares drawings and guides you through the process either way.

faqs.q: My house is a terrace on a narrow street — can you still install?
faqs.a: Usually, yes. Terraced streets are common across the North West and we plan for them as standard. We assess access during your initial survey, coordinate delivery timing and any parking arrangements in advance, and design modules around what your rear access will actually accommodate.

faqs.q: What do North West homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: Open-plan kitchen-diners are the most popular choice, especially for terraces and semis with smaller original kitchens. Home offices, kids' playrooms and garden rooms are close behind — any space your current floor plan doesn't give you, designed and built to suit how your household lives.
